headline: Electronic Arts (EA) After Battlefield 6 News Is The Stock Fully Valued
summary: Electronic Arts (EA) stock was in focus after the company and Battlefield Studios unveiled a major Battlefield 6 and Battlefield REDSEC update featuring naval warfare and a Top Gun crossover, expanding one of EA’s flagship franchises. See our latest analysis for Electronic Arts. Against this backdrop of new game launches and M&A headlines, Electronic Arts’ share price sits at $207.84, with a 30 day share price return of 2.37% and a 1 year total shareholder return of 39.26%, suggesting...

headline: Battlefield 6 and Top Gun Join Forces for Biggest Blockbuster Season Yet
summary: REDWOOD CITY, Calif., July 17, 2026--Electronic Arts Inc. (NASDAQ: EA) and Battlefield Studios today revealed a first look at Battlefield 6™ and Battlefield REDSEC, delivering the franchise's largest seasonal update to date with Naval Warfare, new content, and a meticulously crafted knockout Top Gun crossover experience in partnership with Paramount Games Studio. headline: Electronic Arts (EA) Stock Could Be 28% Overvalued As College Football Backlash Forces Changes
summary: Electronic Arts stock has delivered a 53.8% return over the past three years, but both the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) intrinsic value estimate and market multiple checks currently point to the shares trading at a premium rather than at a clear discount. Electronic Arts' 53.8% three year return highlights strong shareholder gains, which can make fresh entries more sensitive to valuation. The global launch of EA SPORTS College Football 27 and the broader pipeline of live service titles may...

headline: Exclusive-Saudi PIF set to win EU nod for Electronic Arts deal under subsidy rules, sources say
summary: A group of investors including Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund is set ‌to secure European Union approval for its $55 billion acquisition ‌of video game developer Electronic Arts under EU subsidy rules, people familiar with the ​matter said.  Saudi Arabia's $1 trillion wealth fund, Jared Kushner's Affinity Partners and private equity firm Silver Lake announced the deal, the largest leveraged buyout in history, in September last year.  The European Commission, which acts ​as the EU competition enforcer, ‌is set to clear the deal after the end of its preliminary review under its Foreign Subsidies Regulation (FSR) on July 30, the people said.
